questions.text: The probability of an impossible event is:
questions.explanation: Probability of an impossible event is 0. Probability of a certain event is 1.
P(not E) = 1 - P(E). This is because P(E) + P(not E) = 1.

questions.correctAnswer: An event having only one outcome of the experiment.
questions.explanation: Elementary event is an event with only one outcome. Sum of probabilities of all elementary events is 1.

questions.text: The probability of getting a number greater than 4 when a die is thrown is ________.
questions.explanation: Numbers greater than 4 are 5 and 6 (2 outcomes). Total outcomes = 6. P = 2/6 = 1/3.
